proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one binary feature in the dataset is associated with mean pfs_months at p<0.05 (univariate Welch t-test screen across all 80 binary features).
At least one continuous feature in the dataset is correlated with pfs_months at p<0.05 (univariate Pearson correlation screen across the 34 continuous features).
At least one ordinal feature in the dataset is associated with pfs_months (univariate Spearman correlation across 7 small-cardinality integer features).

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: t,p = ttest_ind(y[df[c]==1], y[df[c]==0], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Top binary signals: feature_051 (diff -1.352 mo, p<1e-300), feature_038 (diff +0.972 mo, p=2e-216), feature_013 (diff -0.327, p=2e-58), feature_043 (diff -0.305, p=1e-49), feature_109 (diff -0.228, p=3e-7), feature_067 (diff +0.221, p=7e-4). The strongest binary signal is feature_051.

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols: r,p = pearsonr(df[c], y)
analyses.result_summary: Top continuous signals: feature_078 (r=+0.766, p<1e-300), feature_099 (r=-0.123, p=2e-167), feature_092 (r=+0.102, p=3e-115), feature_009 (r=-0.038, p=3e-17), feature_070 (r=-0.013, p=0.004), feature_006 (r=-0.011, p=0.013). feature_078 dominates among continuous predictors.

analyses.code: for c in ord_cols: rho,p = spearmanr(df[c], y)
analyses.result_summary: feature_057 (3-level: 0/1/2) Spearman rho = -0.360, p<1e-300. Other small-cardinality integer features (feature_071, 025, 026, 075, 096, 033) all p>0.15. Only feature_057 is meaningfully ordinal-prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65) is associated with longer pfs_months in a roughly linear fashion (linear OLS slope > 0).
Patients with feature_051 = 1 (prevalence ~55%) have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with feature_051 = 0, by about one month.
Patients with feature_038 = 1 (prevalence ~20%) have longer mean pfs_months than feature_038 = 0 patients, by about one month.
feature_057 (0/1/2) shows a monotonic dose-response negative association with pfs_months: each higher level reduces mean PFS by ~1 month.
Higher feature_092 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive slope).
Higher feature_099 (continuous, range 0-25) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative slope).

analyses.result_summary: OLS slope = +0.1765 months per unit of feature_078, p<1e-300. Mean PFS rises from 2.08 (lowest quartile) to 6.57 (highest quartile). Effect is large and unambiguous.
feature_051=0: mean PFS 5.055 (n=22519); feature_051=1: mean PFS 3.703 (n=27481). Difference = -1.352 mo, p<1e-300.
feature_038=0: mean PFS 4.117 (n=39978); feature_038=1: mean PFS 5.089 (n=10022). Difference = +0.972 mo, p=2e-216.
feature_057=0: mean 5.271 (n=17592); =1: mean 4.054 (n=24971); =2: mean 2.908 (n=7437). Monotonic decline of ~1.2 mo per level.
Decile means rise monotonically from 3.91 (lowest decile, range 1.5-3.2) to 4.67 (highest decile, range 4.4-5.5). Pearson r = +0.102, p=3e-115. Effect is small per unit but consistent.
Quartile means decline monotonically from 4.57 (0-1.7) to 3.87 (>7.2). Pearson r = -0.123, p=2e-167.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The top eight univariate predictors (feature_078, feature_051, feature_038, feature_057, feature_092, feature_099, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_009, feature_006) each retain independent associations with pfs_months in a multivariable OLS model (each |t|>5).
A multivariable OLS regression on these top features explains a large share of variance in pfs_months (R-squared > 0.80).
After fitting the multivariable model on the top features, the remaining ~110 features add little additional explanatory power (kitchen-sink R-squared barely exceeds the top-feature model R-squared).

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS on the 11 candidates yields R-squared = 0.8584. All retained predictors are highly significant: feature_078 beta=+0.176 (t=453), feature_051 beta=-1.361 (t=-175), feature_057 beta=-1.172 (t=-206), feature_038 beta=+0.943 (t=98), feature_092 beta=+0.470 (t=61), feature_099 beta=-0.076 (t=-76), feature_013 beta=-0.353 (t=-44), feature_043 beta=-0.287 (t=-36), feature_109 beta=-0.355 (t=-19), feature_009 beta=-0.005 (t=-21). feature_067 was not independently significant (p=0.36). Cross-correlations among the 8 strongest predictors are all |r|<0.012, so each contributes orthogonally.

Full kitchen-sink OLS using all 122 numeric features yields R-squared = 0.8589 vs 0.8584 for the 11-feature model. Only ~5 additional features cross p<0.01 thresholds, with effect sizes <0.05 mo. The 11 identified features capture essentially all systematic variance in pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_064 (race: white, hispanic, black, asian, other) -- one-way ANOVA p<0.05.
Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_018 (insurance: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured) -- one-way ANOVA p<0.05.
feature_051's effect on pfs_months is modified by feature_064 (race x feature_051 interaction) -- joint F-test of interaction terms is significant.
feature_078's effect on pfs_months is modified by feature_064 (race x feature_078 interaction) -- joint F-test is significant.

analyses.result_summary: Group means: white 4.316 (n=32456), hispanic 4.315 (n=7572), asian 4.306 (n=3003), black 4.273 (n=5996), other 4.407 (n=973). One-way ANOVA F=0.872, p=0.480. After adjusting for top biological predictors, race coefficients all |t|<0.7, p>0.5. No race effect on PFS.

Group means: private 4.330 (n=19898), medicare 4.312 (n=21105), medicaid 4.277 (n=6991), uninsured 4.249 (n=2006). One-way ANOVA F=1.475, p=0.219. After adjustment, all |t|<1.3, p>0.2. No insurance effect on PFS.

analyses.code: OLS with race dummies + race*feature_051; F-test on interaction terms
analyses.result_summary: Stratum-specific feature_051 effects: white -1.341, hispanic -1.389, asian -1.334, black -1.347, other -1.521 -- all near the pooled estimate of -1.35. Joint F-test for race x feature_051 interaction: F=0.433, p=0.785. No effect modification by race.

analyses.code: OLS with race dummies + race*feature_078; F-test on interaction terms
analyses.result_summary: Joint F-test for race x feature_078 interaction: F=0.540, p=0.706. The strong feature_078 slope (+0.176 mo/unit) is identical across race strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_038 and feature_013 have a multiplicative interaction on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_038 is attenuated when feature_013=1 versus feature_013=0.
feature_038 and feature_043 have a multiplicative interaction on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_038 is attenuated when feature_043=1 versus feature_043=0.
feature_038 and feature_109 have a multiplicative interaction on pfs_months: the benefit of feature_038 is attenuated when feature_109=1 versus feature_109=0.

Treatment-like-feature feature_038 produces a clinically meaningful PFS benefit only in patients negative for all three resistance-marker-like features (feature_013=0 AND feature_043=0 AND feature_109=0); the benefit is essentially zero when any of these is positive.

analyses.result_summary: Cell means: f038=0/f013=0 PFS=4.117 (n=23286); f038=0/f013=1 PFS=4.116 (n=16692); f038=1/f013=0 PFS=5.773 (n=5816); f038=1/f013=1 PFS=4.143 (n=4206). Interaction coefficient = -1.629 mo, p=8e-226. The +1.66 mo benefit of feature_038 in feature_013-negative patients collapses to +0.03 mo in feature_013-positive patients.

Cell means: f038=0/f109=0 PFS=4.116; f038=0/f109=1 PFS=4.126; f038=1/f109=0 PFS=5.144; f038=1/f109=1 PFS=3.971. Interaction coefficient = -1.183 mo, p=6e-23. Despite feature_109's low prevalence (~5%), it consistently abolishes the feature_038 benefit.

analyses.code: any_res = (f013|f043|f109); compare f038 effect within strata
analyses.result_summary: any_resistance=0 stratum (n=17372, 35%): feature_038 mean PFS rises from 4.117 (n=13911) to 6.875 (n=3461), diff=+2.758 mo, p<1e-300. any_resistance=1 stratum (n=32628, 65%): feature_038 mean PFS 4.117 -> 4.147, diff=+0.030 mo, p=0.294. The treatment-like benefit is exclusive to the marker-negative subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Restricting to patients negative for both feature_013 and feature_043, feature_038 still produces a >2 mo PFS benefit; in any cell with at least one of the markers positive, the feature_038 effect is approximately zero.
feature_051 is purely prognostic, not predictive: its negative effect on pfs_months is the same in feature_038=0 and feature_038=1 patients (no feature_051 x feature_038 interaction).
Among the remaining low-prevalence binary features (n1 between 100 and 5000), none produces a positive PFS shift larger than +0.25 mo after the dominant signals are accounted for.

analyses.code: groupby (feature_013, feature_043) and compute feature_038 effect

analyses.result_summary: feature_038=0: feature_051 effect = -1.369 mo (5.185 -> 3.499). feature_038=1: feature_051 effect = -1.300 mo (5.809 -> 4.509). Difference is 0.07 mo, well within noise. feature_051 is a strong prognostic marker but does not modify treatment benefit.

analyses.code: screen low-prev binaries (100<=n1<=5000) for positive PFS diff
analyses.result_summary: Best low-prev positive candidates: feature_067 +0.221 mo (n1=1506, p=7e-4), feature_089 +0.092 mo, feature_001 +0.071 mo, feature_032 +0.058 mo. Only feature_067 reaches a meaningful effect, and it loses significance after adjustment for top features (multivariable p=0.36). No additional low-prevalence treatment-like signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding the feature_038 x (NOT any_resistance) interaction term to the multivariable model, the main effects of feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, and feature_038 alone all collapse toward zero, while the interaction coefficient is large and highly significant (>+2.5 mo).

Among biomarker-eligible patients (any_resistance=0), feature_038 efficacy varies across feature_057 levels (worse performance status reduces treatment benefit) -- significant treatment x feature_057 interaction within the eligible subgroup.
Among biomarker-eligible patients, feature_038 efficacy varies with feature_078 (older patients gain less benefit) -- significant treatment x feature_078 interaction.

analyses.code: OLS with all top features plus feature_038*(1-any_res); R^2 reported
analyses.result_summary: Full model R-squared = 0.9136. After interaction is included: feature_038 main effect coefficient = -0.0025 (p=0.79); feature_013 = -0.001 (p=0.91); feature_043 = +0.002 (p=0.71); feature_109 = +0.003 (p=0.85); the interaction term feature_038*(1-any_res) coefficient = +2.738 (t=178, p<1e-300). Resistance markers and treatment have no main effect once their interaction is modeled - they exist only as effect modifiers.

analyses.code: feature_038 effect by feature_057 within any_res=0 subgroup; OLS interaction
analyses.result_summary: Stratum-specific f038 effect in eligible patients: f057=0: +2.665 mo, f057=1: +2.765 mo, f057=2: +2.743 mo. Formal interaction coefficient = +0.053, p=0.39. Treatment benefit is essentially constant across performance-status-like levels.

analyses.code: OLS y ~ f038 + f078 + f038*f078 within any_res=0
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = +0.0032 mo per unit f078, p=0.29. Treatment benefit does not vary meaningfully across feature_078.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of treatment-like feature_038 differs by feature_064 (race) -- chi-square test on the cross-tabulation is significant.
Receipt of treatment-like feature_038 differs by feature_018 (insurance) -- chi-square test is significant.
Among biomarker-eligible patients (any_res=0), feature_038 receipt differs by race -- a marker of disparity in access to indicated therapy.
Among biomarker-eligible patients, feature_038 receipt differs by insurance.
Prevalence of any of the resistance markers (feature_013, feature_043, feature_109) differs by race.
Among biomarker-eligible patients, the magnitude of feature_038's benefit on pfs_months differs across race categories.

analyses.result_summary: Treatment receipt rates: white 20.0%, asian 21.1%, black 20.1%, hispanic 19.8%, other 19.2%. Chi-square = 3.045, p=0.550. No racial difference in treatment receipt.
Treatment receipt: medicare 20.2%, private 20.1%, medicaid 19.8%, uninsured 19.0%. Chi-square = 1.913, p=0.591. No insurance-based difference in treatment receipt.

analyses.code: chi2 on feature_038 vs race within any_res=0
analyses.result_summary: Among biomarker-eligible: white 19.9%, asian 21.4%, black 20.4%, hispanic 19.3%, other 18.5%. Chi-square = 2.729, p=0.604. No disparity in indicated treatment receipt.

analyses.code: chi2 on feature_038 vs insurance within any_res=0
analyses.result_summary: Among biomarker-eligible: medicare 19.7%, private 20.2%, medicaid 19.9%, uninsured 19.4%. Chi-square = 0.903, p=0.825. No disparity by insurance.

any_res prevalence by race: white 65.3%, asian 65.6%, black 64.7%, hispanic 65.8%, other 62.8%. Range only 3 percentage points; chi-square equivalent statistic shows no meaningful difference. Individual marker prevalences (feature_013: 41-43%, feature_043: 34-36%, feature_109: 4.3-5.2%) are also nearly identical across races.

analyses.code: compare f038 effect within feature_064 strata, in any_res=0 only
analyses.result_summary: Treatment benefit in eligible patients by race: white +2.630 mo, hispanic +3.207 mo, asian +2.884 mo, black +2.786 mo, other +3.070 mo. All p<1e-10; within-stratum point estimates overlap and the average is +2.7-3.2 mo. No clinically meaningful disparity in treatment efficacy across races.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057's negative effect on pfs_months is uniform across race categories (no feature_057 x feature_064 effect modification).
After adjusting for the dominant 12-term model, no remaining binary feature has a residual effect larger than 0.05 mo with p<0.001.
feature_009 (long-tail continuous, range 0.02-777) shows a stronger linear association with pfs_months on a log-transformed scale than on its raw scale.

analyses.result_summary: feature_057 effect across race strata: white (5.27/4.06/2.91), hispanic (5.28/4.07/2.89), asian (5.21/4.01/3.03), black (5.26/3.99/2.87), other (5.48/4.13/2.84). Step from level 0 to 2 is ~2.4 mo decline in every race -- uniform.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on residuals from the 12-term model, for each remaining binary
analyses.result_summary: Top residual binary effects: feature_087 +0.025 (p=0.005), feature_041 -0.026 (p=0.005), feature_086 -0.018 (p=0.007), feature_052 +0.022 (p=0.009). All effect sizes are <0.03 mo, well below clinical relevance, and none survives Bonferroni correction across ~80 binary tests (alpha = 6.25e-4). No additional clinically meaningful binary signal exists.

Pearson r on raw scale = -0.038 (p=3e-17). On log1p-transformed scale: r = -0.062 (p=5e-44). Quintile means decline monotonically (4.50 -> 4.45 -> 4.33 -> 4.14 -> 4.14). Log transform improves linear fit, consistent with a tumor-marker-style distribution where extreme high values flatten.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A 12-term OLS model containing feature_078, feature_051, feature_057, feature_092, feature_099, feature_009, feature_006, feature_013, feature_043, feature_109, feature_038, and the feature_038*(no-resistance-marker) interaction explains >90% of variance in pfs_months in this 50000-patient cohort.

Across all the disparity-related tests in iterations 4 and 8 (race main effect, insurance main effect, race x feature_051, race x feature_078, treatment receipt by race, treatment receipt by insurance, marker prevalence by race, treatment efficacy by race, treatment efficacy by insurance), none reaches the threshold of clinical or statistical significance.

analyses.result_summary: Final model R-squared = 0.9136. Coefficient summary: feature_078 +0.176 (t=581), feature_051 -1.357 (t=-224), feature_057 -1.167 (t=-262), feature_092 +0.476 (t=79), feature_099 -0.077 (t=-99), feature_009 -0.005 (t=-27), feature_006 -0.0005 (t=-14), feature_013/043/109 main effects ~0 (p>0.7), feature_038 main effect ~0 (p=0.79), and feature_038*(no_resistance) interaction = +2.738 (t=178). Twelve features capture essentially all systematic variance in pfs_months.

analyses.code: summary across iter 4 and iter 8 demographic tests
analyses.result_summary: All nine demographic-disparity tests are null: race ANOVA p=0.48, insurance ANOVA p=0.22, race x feature_051 F-test p=0.79, race x feature_078 F-test p=0.71, race x treatment-receipt chi-square p=0.55, insurance x treatment-receipt p=0.59, race x marker prevalence essentially equal, race-stratified treatment effect range 2.6-3.2 (overlapping), insurance-stratified treatment effect range 2.6-3.1 (overlapping). No demographic disparities in either treatment access or treatment efficacy in this cohort.
